decision: Claude Code is the coordinator and Codex is the implementation and review layer, connected through the codex-plugin-cc bridge with its automatic stop-review feature turned off
rationale: Chosen at D8 for org-wide consistency of AI usage; risk accepted knowingly with two mitigations planned: background delegation and a documented fallback mode

decision: Approach C selected: pilot-extracted harness — week-one bootstrap wedge (forge CLI, dual-runtime layout, sign-off gate, dual-runtime linter, harness.yaml), then one real client project with a named champion dev
rationale: Demand evidence is a leadership mandate rather than bottom-up pull; champion-led pilots are the documented remedy for mandate-driven platform adoption failure

decision: kl-engg-constitution merges into symphony-forge as constitution/ via one-time import; Notion pages deprecated; symphony-forge is the single source of truth for standards, changed via PR only; a thin pointer shim skill remains for non-forge repos until they migrate
rationale: Repo files are readable by both runtimes, and PR-based changes give every standards edit a diff, review, and rationale

decision: Layout centered on a universal .agents/ directory holding all runtime-neutral agent substance (phase prompts, role definitions, hook logic, deterministic scripts); .codex/ and .claude/ become thin registration adapters, enforced by a linter rule
rationale: User request at D14: .agents/ is the emerging universal convention; neither runtime discovers it natively so thin adapters remain mandatory plumbing

decision: Hooks redesigned to be quiet by default and loud only at phase gates: context injection once per session, silence unless a phase-transition violation, no keyword or exit-code or turn-end triggers; the prompt-keyword sniffer, per-command context injection, non-zero-exit rule, and turn-end artifact requirement are removed
rationale: Audit found the current hooks inject noise on every prompt and every command, have false-positive triggers on routine non-zero exits, and duplicate the pr_ready gate in a way that can loop with codex-plugin-cc

decision: Review phase owned by the three existing Codex review subagents (quality, performance, security) with autoreview-style loop discipline added to their prompts; openclaw autoreview reserved as an escalation tier at PR-ready for security-sensitive, migration, or cross-domain tasks, optionally as a codex+claude panel; the two review loops never run on the same task
rationale: Subagents are standards-aware and already emit the three hard-gate artifacts; autoreview is deliberately isolated from repo conventions so its value is independence, which fits escalation not routine gating

decision: During the planning phase, codebase exploration is delegated to Codex: the Claude Code planner dispatches read-only Codex investigations and plans from the returned findings rather than exploring the code itself
rationale: Extends the coordinator-executor split into planning; Codex traces code paths carefully, Claude context stays lean for synthesis, and exploration findings become citable reports

decision: Task planning gains a sanctioned alternative: Claude Code plan mode remains the default planner, and the planner-high Codex agent is allowed when a dev prefers it, under the identical contract (planner.md sections, plan save artifact, decision records, gates)
rationale: User relaxation of the earlier structural-split decision for the planning phase only; the gates check the artifact rather than the author, so runtime choice is safe

decision: PR 2 (stage-based orchestration and factory validation automation, authored April 2026) closed unmerged as superseded by the v2 harness: its script directory moved to .agents/, its hooks were removed by the de-noising decision, and its goals are delivered by the gate precondition matrix, the test suite, forge next, and harness.yaml
rationale: Merging a three-month-old branch across the architecture migration would resurrect the old layout and fail the dual-runtime linter; salvageable ideas go through the evolution loop instead

decision: The harness runtime stack is Claude Code plus Codex only; all references to a third orchestration layer were removed from the docs, manifest, and prompts, and the escalation review tool identifier is plain autoreview
rationale: User decision: two runtimes are the deliberate, complete stack; a placeholder third mode was doc surface with no artifact behind it
